summary: Iteration 4 testing of streak milestone celebrations + at-risk alerts. Backend: 21/21 new pytest cases pass + 32/32 regression (test_new_features.py + test_memory_link.py) = 53/53 total. New /api/streak fields atRisk and newMilestone verified. /api/streak/celebrate idempotently marks milestone as celebrated; subsequent /api/streak returns newMilestone=null. /api/streak/save creates partner notification with type='streak_save'. Streak count correctly reflects photo uploads (seeded Alice/Bob couple = 3 days). Frontend Playwright E2E: StreakBadge renders with count=3, MilestoneCelebration modal appears with correct emoji/title/subtitle for milestone=3 (🌱 + '3 Days!' + "You're building something special"), modal close button dismisses overlay and POSTs /api/streak/celebrate (verified by reload — modal does not reappear), save-streak-button hidden because both partners sent today (atRisk=false). All data-testids present and working: streak-badge, streak-count, save-streak-button, milestone-overlay, milestone-card, milestone-emoji, milestone-title, milestone-subtitle, milestone-close-button.

minor.issue: atRisk gate uses two different hour thresholds in code: server.py uses hour>=14 (2pm UTC), scheduler.py uses 18<=hour<=22 (6-10pm UTC). Inconsistent — UI may show atRisk=true between 2pm-6pm UTC but scheduler won't actually send the alert until 6pm. Either align both to same window or document the divergence.
Accepts arbitrary milestone integer (e.g., 7777) and stores it in celebratedMilestones. No validation against MILESTONES list. Low impact since UI controls the value, but worth restricting.

action_items: Optional: Align at-risk hour threshold between /api/streak (>=14 UTC) and scheduler._check_streak_at_risk (18-22 UTC).
Optional: Validate /api/streak/celebrate milestone param against MILESTONES list.
Carryover from iter3: server.py is 762 lines — split into routers/ (auth, photos, messages, settings, streak, memories).
critical_code_review_comments: Scheduler is_at_risk window (6pm-10pm UTC) differs from API at-risk window (>=2pm UTC). Pick one source of truth.

StreakBadge polls /api/streak every 30s — fine, but newMilestone state could re-show modal if user reloads before celebrate POST completes. Mitigated by shownMilestonesLocally Set, but only in-memory.
celebratedMilestones is stored as an array in couples doc; using $addToSet for idempotency is correct.
MilestoneCelebration modal renders 30 confetti emoji DOM nodes with framer-motion — minor perf cost, acceptable for one-off celebration.

/api/streak/celebrate does not require the milestone to actually match the current streak — a user could pre-celebrate milestone=1000 to suppress it forever. Consider only allowing celebration for the current streak value (or a value <= current streak).
test_credentials: No auth. Seeded users Alice (id=52d6b731-430d-42eb-9da2-f18674f02bdc) and Bob (id=9f07549e-1b24-46f8-ab0f-932ab700bc8a), coupleId=c9f6f897-cf1a-477a-97bd-0d26176a58ca, 3-day streak.
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_I4_* users (Alice/Bob, lonely, nocouple, streaksolo, S_A, S_B). Reset celebratedMilestones=[] on seeded couple at start of frontend test, then frontend test re-celebrated milestone=3 by closing the modal. Note: celebratedMilestones may need to be reset again for next iteration if testing milestone modal.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `export REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) && cd /app && pytest backend/tests/ -v`. All 53 tests should pass. For frontend, login as Alice (set localStorage.userId=52d6b731-430d-42eb-9da2-f18674f02bdc). To re-test MilestoneCelebration modal, reset celebratedMilestones in mongo: db.couples.update_one({id: COUPLE_ID}, {$set: {celebratedMilestones: []}}). The at-risk scheduler path (_check_streak_at_risk) is hour-gated 18-22 UTC and could not be exercised live without time mocking — endpoint-level at-risk logic in /api/streak was verified via direct shape checks (atRisk=False when both sent today).
rca of the issue: No bugs found in iteration 4 features. The only divergence worth noting: server.py /api/streak uses hour>=14 UTC for atRisk while scheduler.py _check_streak_at_risk gates between 18 and 22 UTC, creating a 4-hour window (14-18 UTC) where UI shows at-risk but no push goes out. Not blocking — UI 'Ping!' button still works because save-streak-button is shown based on atRisk and user-only ping is a synchronous user action, not scheduler-driven.
